title: selinum anthriscus
intro: Selinum anthriscus, also known as Wild Celery or Wild Parsley, is a plant species in the carrot family that resembles a mix of parsley and celery in appearance. The medicinal properties of Selinum anthriscus include its carminative, diuretic, and expectorant effects, making it useful for treating digestive issues and respiratory problems. The plant is also believed to have various health benefits, including reducing inflammation, improving digestion, and providing antioxidant properties. Herbal preparations of Selinum anthriscus include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to alleviate symptoms of colds, coughs, and other respiratory issues.
